# sim.simulate_admixture_source_gene_flow { #tspaint.sim.simulate_admixture_source_gene_flow }

```python
tspaint.sim.simulate_admixture_source_gene_flow(
    n_admix=10,
    n_ref=10,
    sequence_length=2000000.0,
    recombination_rate=1e-08,
    ploidy=2,
    random_seed=42,
    mutation_rate=None,
    **demography_kwargs,
)
```

Admixed queries + A/B references where the ``SOURCE_A`` panel carries real ``SOURCE_B``
introgressed tracts from a prior A↔B pulse (:func:`admixture_demography_source_gene_flow`).

The ``SOURCE_A`` references are the contaminated panel (nominally A, ``~prev_migration`` B);
the ``SOURCE_B`` references are pure. Returns a :class:`Simulation` (``.ts`` / ``.queries`` /
``.labels`` / ``.truth_states`` / ``.sample_sets``); the query truth is the proximate A / B source.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**n_admix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of admixed (query) individuals.

<code>[**n_ref**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [10]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of individuals sampled from **each** of the two sources (A and B).

<code>[**sequence_length**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`simulate_admixture`.

<code>[**recombination_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`simulate_admixture`.

<code>[**ploidy**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`simulate_admixture`.

<code>[**mutation_rate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`simulate_admixture`.

<code>[**random_seed**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[optional](`optional`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2000000.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   As for :func:`simulate_admixture`.

<code>[****demography_kwargs**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} []{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [{}]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Passed to :func:`admixture_demography_source_gene_flow` (e.g. ``T_admix``, ``T_split``, ``T_prev_admix``, ``prev_migration``, ``f_A``, ``Ne``).

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Simulation](`tspaint.sim.Simulation`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A :class:`~tspaint.sim.Simulation`; its default ``.labels`` panel pairs the contaminated ``SOURCE_A`` references (nominally A, carrying ~``prev_migration`` genuine B tracts) with the pure ``SOURCE_B`` references, and each query's ``.truth_states`` is its proximate A / B source.
